differentials.name: Highly vascular neoplasm: hepatocellular carcinoma (10-15%), hepatic angiosarcoma, metastases (lung carcinoma, renal cell carcinoma, melanoma), hepatic adenoma (!) Risk factors:   large size of mass; peripheral / subcapsular location; ⇑ vascularity Pathophysiology:   invasion of vessels; ⇑ intratumoral pressure; venous congestion ← venous thrombosis; lack of autoregulation of tumor vessels
differentials.raw: Highly vascular neoplasm: hepatocellular carcinoma (10-15%), hepatic angiosarcoma, metastases (lung carcinoma, renal cell carcinoma, melanoma), hepatic adenoma (!) Risk factors:   large size of mass; peripheral / subcapsular location; ⇑ vascularity Pathophysiology:   invasion of vessels; ⇑ intratumoral pressure; venous congestion ← venous thrombosis; lack of autoregulation of tumor vessels

differentials.name: Anticoagulation
differentials.raw: Anticoagulation (more commonly bleeding into psoas / rectus muscle)

context: viral infection (CMV, malaria, EBV); congenital disease; metabolic abnormality (Gaucher disease, amyloidosis); neoplasm (leukemia, lymphoma, hemangiomatosis, angiosarcoma) hemorrhagic ovarian cyst (usually corpus luteal / follicular cyst); ectopic pregnancy; HELLP syndrome; endometriosis; uterine rupture ruptured arterial aneurysm (abdominal aorta, splenic artery, hepatic artery); ruptured pseudoaneurysm (of hepatic, splenic, gastroduodenal artery) in pancreatitis (b)  iatrogenic
